content.full_text:
February 3, 1992

Blanca Breeze, Esquire
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Biggs-West Gridley Water District
Your File No. A91-023

Dear Ms. Breeze:

On February 7, 1991, you responded to our request for advice regarding the duties and responsibilities of members of the board of directors of the Biggs-West Gridley Water District under the provisions of the Political Reform Act. We asked whether members of the board of directors of BWGWD could participate in decisions regarding water delivery reductions. You responded that all five members of the board of directors of the District must disqualify themselves and abstain from participating in decisions regarding water delivery reductions except to the extent their participation is legally required.

As a result of the fact that the board members are legally required to make decisions, you provided a mechanism by which the board may bring back as many disqualified members as is necessary to establish a quorum. The selection process for this quorum must be by lot or other means of random selection.

Based upon that advice, the board disqualified all five members and then, using a random process of selection, requalified three board members to establish a quorum. Once that quorum was established, those board members were deemed a "special board" and were the only members of the board of directors who participated in the decision. This participation was done without regard to the possible effect of the decision upon the "special board" members' financial interests.

Since that advice, the makeup of the District board of directors has changed as a result of the November 1991 general district election. Two of the members of the special board were replaced during the election by two additional board members.

In 1992, the District faces the same question as was before the District in 1991. We intend to use the same advice which you

Blanca Breeze, FPPC  
Re: Biggs-West Gridley Water District  
Your File No. A91-023  
February 3, 1992

provided previously. It is our intention to advise the board that all five members of the board are disqualified for the same reasons as set forth in your previous advice letter. Thereafter, we will advise the board that it is legally required to make a decision and, consequently, the minimum number to establish a quorum -- three members -- will be requalified using a random process as set forth in your advice letter.

In the event that your advice now would be inconsistent with the advice you previously provided to us, please advise. Otherwise, we will continue to use the procedure you previously provided for us for decision-making in 1992.

Very truly yours,

MINASIAN, MINASIAN, MINASIAN, SPRUANCE, BABER, MEITH & SOARES

BY [signature]
MICHAEL V. SEXTON

MVS:aw  
cc: Biggs-West Gridley Water District
